section.heading: Processing payment transactions by financial institutions
section.chapeau: To the extent that an entity is engaged in activities of a financial institution (as defined in section 3401 of title 12), or is engaged in authorizing, processing, clearing, settling, billing, transferring, reconciling, or collecting payments, for a financial institution, this part, and any standard adopted under this part, shall not apply to the entity with respect to such activities, including the following:

paragraph.content: The use or disclosure of information by the entity for authorizing, processing, clearing, settling, billing, transferring, reconciling or collecting, a payment for, or related to, health plan premiums or health care, where such payment is made by any means, including a credit, debit, or other payment card, an account, check, or electronic funds transfer.

paragraph.chapeau: The request for, or the use or disclosure of, information by the entity with respect to a payment described in paragraph (1)—

clause.content: an inquiry from, or to, a customer;

subparagraph.content: in a communication to a customer of the entity regarding the customer’s transactions, payment card, account, check, or electronic funds transfer;
for reporting to consumer reporting agencies; or

clause.content: a civil or criminal subpoena; or
a Federal or State law regulating the entity.
